text: we actually attain that goal now in the military and also as a Trader we need to adapt a basian style of thinking basian analysis is where we update our understanding of things as new knowledge comes out so one of the best possible ways you can be adaptable as a Trader is you focus on the process and far less on the outcome that's one of those really weird things that it sounds like means nothing but it really is the way to find success long-term as a Trader it's where you are less concerned with how each individual trade comes out in the wash and we focus so much more on executing a sound process because in the long run your returns as a Trader literally are a measurement of your ability to adhere and execute a sound process that's positively expectant risk management we use a lot of risk Management in the military and it's because we do a lot of risky things the main thing I want you to pay attention to from a military perspective two things the first one is what we call operational risk management we use something called a ram risk assessment Matrix what we try to do is we have two columns we have the probability of something occurring and then the severity that allows us to understand what kind of controls need to be put in place to put this in the context of a Trader if you're putting on

title: Using Military Strategies in Options Trading
text: we have two columns we have the probability of something occurring and then the severity that allows us to understand what kind of controls need to be put in place to put this in the context of a Trader if you're putting on a trade that has a very low probability but if that probability happens it wipes out your account guess what it still is important enough that you have to manage it so the idea of a risk assessment Matrix and this kind of process is we're trying to frame risk in a way that we're not accepting unwarranted risks and that the risks that we are accepting have a commensurate reward so the way that you can do that as a Trader is literally by developing your own RAM and you can use things like a trade checklist you can employ portfolio level circuit breakers which I have an entire video that talks about all these riskmanagement approaches as a Trader we'll link to that below that you can then think through all of these things at a time in a trade management plan and then avoid taking stupid risks that you don't even know are there like that one time you you sold puts on a company because it had IV and you didn't know that it had earnings coming out that kind of thing the last one after Action reviews so you'll absolutely have heard me refer to

title: Using Military Strategies in Options Trading
text: you sold puts on a company because it had IV and you didn't know that it had earnings coming out that kind of thing the last one after Action reviews so you'll absolutely have heard me refer to this before it's called an A and we ask ourselves a few questions what was supposed to happen what actually happened why did it happen what can we do better next time and literally that exact thing applies to you as a Trader the way that I conduct this a process is I think of it following individual TR I conduct a weekly mini a look over the entire portfolio I do a monthly deep a it's typically like several hours 3 to four hours of combing through everything identifying Trends and then every single year I have a holistic AR that literally takes me like almost two weeks that I go through everything I did but this process of conducting an a in a structured way leads to finding things that you can employ smarter going forward this is exactly how you apply military strategies and decision-making to trading options see you later
